title: IaP Price Snapshot v1
description: A versioned, content-addressed capture of provider list prices (spec ch. 16 §16.1, IEP-0005). Snapshots are the ONLY pricing input at computation time — never live lookups — so cost reports are byte-reproducible from a snapshot id. Produced/refreshed out of band (e.g. via MCP pricing sources), then vendored.

id.description: Human-readable snapshot identity (e.g. reference-cloud-2026-07-01). Combined with the content hash to form the report's priceSnapshot reference.

provider.description: The pricing domain these list prices belong to (e.g. reference-cloud, aws, gcp).

currency.description: ISO 4217 currency code; one currency per snapshot.

region.description: The region/geography these prices apply to (e.g. us-east-1, global).

asOf.description: RFC 3339 pricing timestamp — when these list prices were captured.

source.description: Provenance of the prices (a catalog URL, an MCP source id, or an explicit note such as 'illustrative').

prices.description: SKU key → unit price. SKU keys are opaque to the engine; a cost model references them and the engine multiplies quantities by amounts. A SKU absent here makes any resource that needs it visibly unpriced (never guessed).

unit.description: The billing unit the amount is per (e.g. instance-hour, gib-month, million-messages).

amount.description: List price per unit in the snapshot currency.
